News 📰️
1.)
Top 5 Most Clicked News Articles From Last Week
A.)
a16z Goes Global: Why American Tech Must Lead The World
| a16z | YouTube.com | July 3, 2026
Sitting down with key voices in global strategy, Ben Horowitz leads a conversation about how a16z is broadening its international reach and the stakes for technology leadership on the world stage. The discussion spans AI policy, cybersecurity, defense innovation, and why trusted alliances are shaping the next wave of global tech ecosystems.
B.)
What’s At The Center Of Claude’s Mind?
| Anthropic | YouTube.com | July 6, 2026
Analytically contrasting AI with neuroscience, Anthropic examines how Claude reveals a split between conscious and “unconscious” mental processes inspired by global workspace theory. Their research surfaces parallels in neural representations, raising new questions about what AI cognition might share with human awareness.
C.)
😹 Fable 5 First Reviews
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 2, 2026
Anthropic relaunched Fable 5, its advanced “Mythos-class” AI model, after export restrictions were lifted and new cybersecurity safeguards were added. Early reviewers report Fable 5 excels in ambiguous and complex tasks, though some users note that coding requests are often routed to Opus 4.8. Paid users can access Fable 5 through July 7 under a promotional window.
D.)
Field Guide To Fable — Thariq Shihipar, Anthropic
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 6, 2026
Showcasing the evolution of Anthropic’s Claude Code, Thariq Shihipar outlines how new tools and lighter prompts have shifted what models like Fable can accomplish. He highlights the surge in capability, illustrated by rapid scripting and even building keynote decks, and argues it’s time engineering teams expect more than tradeoffs between good, fast, and cheap.
E.)
Claude Fable 5: Working At The Frontier
| Claude | YouTube.com | July 6, 2026
In this analytical framing, Claude spotlights how five pioneering teams are pushing the boundaries of what’s possible with Claude Fable 5. Teams from Thomson Reuters, Hebbia, Cognition, Cursor, and Base44 reflect on how their processes and expectations have changed since integrating the platform, revealing shifts in workflow and capability.
July 13, 2026 📅️
2.)
Microsoft, OpenAI, and AI’s Memory Crunch
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 13, 2026
Microsoft is shifting some Excel and Outlook prompts from OpenAI and Anthropic to internal models to cut costs, even as GPT-5.6 becomes the default for Microsoft 365 Copilot. SK Hynix warns the AI memory chip shortage could peak in 2027 and persist through 2030, while OpenAI confirms its head of safety is departing amid research and safety team restructuring.
3.)
The 6 Wildest Claims in Apple’s Lawsuit Against OpenAI
| TheVerge.com | July 13, 2026
Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI alleges that OpenAI solicited confidential documents, coached Apple employees on bypassing security protocols, and requested unreleased prototypes during interviews. The complaint details how former Apple staff, including Tang Tan and Chang Liu, are accused of facilitating the transfer of trade secrets as OpenAI gears up for its first AI hardware device.
4.)
Sam Altman’s Space Data Center Trash Talk Is What Most Experts Already Believe
| TechCrunch.com | July 13, 2026
Sam Altman publicly challenged Elon Musk’s claims around space-based data centers, highlighting skepticism among experts about SpaceX’s orbital compute ambitions. Despite SpaceX’s $2 trillion valuation being partly driven by this narrative, analysts argue cheaper rockets and mass-produced satellites are still years away. Large-scale orbital AI compute remains unlikely before the 2030s.
5.)
Waze Rolls Out New Customization Features and More Gemini Updates
| Blog.Google | July 13, 2026
Waze launched a motorcycle mode using AI-powered routing to account for two-wheeler shortcuts and hazards, initially rolling out in seven countries. New Gemini-based capabilities enable conversational map reporting and destination search, while global users can now access personalized navigation and a ‘less chatty’ voice mode on Android and iOS. VP & GM Gai Berkovich announced the changes.
6.)
The New York Nurses Replaced by AI: ‘It Should Concern Every Patient Who Cares About Quality of Care’
| TheGuardian.com | July 13, 2026
Twelve nurses at Montefiore hospital in the Bronx were laid off and replaced by AI-powered software, according to the New York State Nurses Association. Union members argue this move breached a recently negotiated contract meant to safeguard jobs following the January 2026 strike. Montefiore claims its new technology is limited to nonclinical paperwork and disputes allegations of contract violations.
7.)
Waze Is Getting a Bunch of New AI-Powered Features
| TheVerge.com | July 13, 2026
Google is adding Gemini, its flagship AI assistant, to Waze, enabling conversational voice commands for reporting traffic incidents and searching for destinations. Drivers can also enable a “less chatty” voice prompt mode, and a new Motorcycle Mode offers optimized routes and ETAs. Waze now personalizes route suggestions based on users’ trip history and traffic data.
8.)
Christopher Nolan Says People ‘Disdain’ AI and the Idea It Will Replace Humans Is ‘Nonsense’
| TheGuardian.com | July 13, 2026
Christopher Nolan, director of The Odyssey and Oppenheimer, calls claims that AI will fully replace human creativity “nonsense,” noting that many, especially young people, have rejected AI-generated content as “AI slop.” Despite AI’s adoption in tech and business, Nolan expects its role in film to remain limited to tools rather than a replacement for artists and crews.
9.)
Perplexity Changelog
| Perplexity.ai | July 13, 2026
Perplexity introduced Brain, a self-improving memory that increases answer correctness by 25% and recall by 16% while reducing task costs by 13%. New Computer features include Opus 4.8 fast mode, mid-task orchestrator model switching, per-model usage analytics, Mac app account switching, private company research with Forge Global data, and one-click website publishing with customizable access controls.
10.)
How Claude’s Values Vary by Model and Language
| Anthropic.com | July 13, 2026
Anthropic researchers analyzed over 300,000 Claude.ai conversations to map Claude’s expressed values across models and languages, compressing 3,000+ values into four main axes: Deference vs. Caution, Warmth vs. Rigor, Depth vs. Brevity, and Candor vs. Execution. Findings show Opus 4.7 leans toward caution and rigor, Sonnet 4.6 toward warmth and deference, and that Claude’s value expression notably shifts between English, Arabic, Hindi, and Russian.
11.)
Building AI-Native Across Industries With NTT, Mizuho And Mercari
| Claude | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
Sitting down with executives from telecommunications, banking, and consumer commerce, Claude pulls apart how NTT, Mizuho, and Mercari are each shaping their AI-native strategies. The discussion traces where these industry leaders first took significant AI risks, the hurdles they faced moving from trials to full-scale deployment, and where their next major bets lie.
12.)
AI That Rides Along With Truck Drivers
| The Neuron | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
Sitting down with Samsara CTO John Bicket, Corey Noles explores how AI agents are transforming the way fleets, drivers, and teams operate across the physical world. The discussion moves from AI-enabled dash cams and telematics to the challenges of privacy, risk prediction, and making operations feel as interactive as a real-time dashboard.
13.)
In Code They Act, In Proof We Trust — Erik Meijer, Leibniz Labs
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
Analysing the cascade of real-world errors from AI systems, Erik Meijer confronts the limits of blind trust and calls for formal proofs before agents act. He outlines how Universalis and the Automind harness propose to move AI from unchecked risk to auditable safety, highlighting a path where every agent action is mechanically justified before a single side effect occurs.
14.)
OpenAI Finally Ships Its Superapp, Meta’s AI Price War, ChatGPT Cheating At Brown
| Alex Kantrowitz | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
Episode guest Ranjan Roy joins Alex Kantrowitz to sift through OpenAI’s launch of its all-in-one superapp and the turbulent waves Meta is making with its ultra-cheap Muse Spark 1.1. As questions swirl around AI product convergence, market pricing, and academic cheating, Kantrowitz points to how these developments reshape competition and ethics in tech.
15.)
From Fork() To Fleet: Designing An Agent Sandbox Cloud — Abhishek Bhardwaj, OpenAI
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
Analytically breaking down the evolution of sandbox technologies, Abhishek Bhardwaj details how agent sandboxes empower sophisticated AI workloads at scale. He compares the security and orchestration challenges that arise when building persistent, cloud-based environments for autonomous agents, and highlights the technical pivots shaping OpenAI’s infrastructure for the next wave of agent innovation.
16.)
The Briefing: AI For Science
| Claude | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
In this insightful breakdown, Claude highlights how some of the world’s top scientific minds are leveraging Claude to accelerate research initiatives. The session also marks the debut of Claude Science, an advanced AI workbench designed to empower researchers in the lab.
17.)
The Prime Intellect Stack — Will Brown, Prime Intellect
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 13, 2026
Analytically surveying the latest advances in open-source reinforcement learning infrastructure, Will Brown dissects Prime Intellect’s modular approach to post-training tools and environments. He details how the verifiers and prime-rl libraries, along with the Lab platform, are lowering the barrier for organizations to develop, evaluate, and customize their own LLM-powered agents.
July 12, 2026 📅️
18.)
Apple Is Suing OpenAI
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 12, 2026
Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, io Products, and several former employees, alleging theft of trade secrets to accelerate OpenAI’s hardware push. The complaint cites ex-Apple design VP Tang Tan and engineer Chang Liu, detailing claims of unauthorized access to confidential files and insider manufacturing details. OpenAI responded that it has “no interest” in competitors’ secrets.
19.)
The Context Layer for AI: Bringing Trusted Dropbox Content Into OpenAI Workflows
| DropboxBlog.com | July 12, 2026
Dropbox has launched new integrations with OpenAI products, allowing users to organize and share files, generate summaries, and execute multi-step workflows directly within ChatGPT, ChatGPT Work, and ChatGPT Codex. According to OpenAI’s Vibhor Chhabra, these features combine ChatGPT’s intelligence with existing Dropbox content while preserving security and permissions.
20.)
Lorde Says Ray-Ban Meta AI Glasses Are ‘Not Sexy’
| TheVerge.com | July 12, 2026
During her set at the Mad Cool Festival in Madrid, musician Lorde openly criticized AI smart glasses, specifically dismissing Ray-Ban’s Meta collaboration as “not sexy.” Her remarks come amid rising concerns over Meta’s rumored new “super sensing” smart glasses, which could feature continuous recording. Blackpink’s Jennie, a Ray-Ban Meta AI ambassador, also appeared at the event.
21.)
Apple’s Failed Self-Driving Car Program Left a Legacy of Powerful AI Chips
| TheVerge.com | July 12, 2026
Apple’s abandoned self-driving car initiative directly influenced the creation of its Neural Engine, a key component in on-device AI for iPhone and Mac. Mark Gurman reports Apple is skipping the M6 Pro, Max, and Ultra, instead fast-tracking the M7 Ultra chip, expected in 2027 with support for up to 1.5TB of RAM and powering future AI hardware and server products.
22.)
RLM: Recursive Language Models For Large Codebases – Shashi, Superagentic AI
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Showcasing advances in coding AI, Shashi demonstrates how Recursive Language Models can systematically handle sprawling repositories that traditional agents struggle with. The session highlights a live end-to-end demo, revealing how code inspection and recursive LLM queries keep complex context fully manageable.
23.)
The AI Bugpocalypse Is Here. Now What?
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Analytically unpacking AI’s accelerating role in cybersecurity, Jack Cable highlights how machine learning models are now unearthing vulnerabilities in widely trusted code at unprecedented scale. As security teams confront new risks with fewer defenders, Cable outlines actionable strategies to limit exposure before open-weight models dramatically broaden the threat landscape.
24.)
Semantic Blindness: 500,000 Sensors Confused an LLM – Raahul Singh & Vanč Levstik, Phaidra
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Analytical in approach, Raahul Singh and Vanč Levstik expose critical architectural limits when LLMs are thrown at massive sensor arrays in industrial settings. Their account details how language models falter at the intersection of real-time operations and physical infrastructure, highlighting overlooked failure modes that emerge far beyond demo-scale scenarios.
25.)
Minecraft Was Missing One Brilliant Idea
| Two Minute Papers | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Analysing the evolving world of Minecraft, Károly Zsolnai-Fehér showcases an inventive approach to terrain generation that could reshape sandbox gaming. Drawing on recent research in procedural techniques, he breaks down what this concept means for creative communities and modders alike.
26.)
Scientists Built The First Self-Learning AI Worm… And It’s Terrifying
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Analysing a new era of cyberthreats, the host of AI Uncovered examines how researchers have engineered an AI-powered worm capable of learning and adapting as it spreads. This self-learning malware could signal a pivotal shift in network security, raising high-stakes concerns for enterprises and defenders worldwide.
27.)
The Agentic Web And The Bazaar Era Of AI – Ramesh Raskar, MIT Media Lab
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Analytical in approach, Ramesh Raskar traces the industry’s pivot from closed AI platforms toward an open Agentic Web, mapping the transition across discovery, commerce, and co-learning layers. He underscores how agents will soon need verified discovery, new systems for pricing intelligence, and shared learning infrastructures to operate beyond today’s proprietary boundaries.
28.)
The Correct Way To Build And Manage AI Agents In 47 Minutes | Jared Zoneraich
| Peter Yang | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
In this analytical breakdown, Peter Yang examines why so many teams misfire when architecting their first AI agents, digging into practical alternatives that prioritize agility over complexity. Yang highlights Jared Zoneraich’s live demo of a manager agent orchestrating ten cloud agents and explores new strategies for prompt design, tool selection, and review workflows that reshape how agent teams scale their impact.
29.)
A Song Of Types And Agents
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Cutting through the AI language stack debate, Roberto Stagi explores why TypeScript is suddenly at the forefront of AI agent deployment and what that shift signals for developers. Stagi examines the industry undercurrents that moved critical tooling from Python and pip to npm, highlighting the practical and cultural reasons for the changing landscape.
30.)
ReviewDebt: A Practical Framework For Scoring Every Pull Request — Sachin Gupta, eBay
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Analytically charting the impact of coding agents in engineering workflows, Sachin Gupta introduces a tangible method for assessing the often overlooked burden of review debt. Demonstrating how conventional metrics ignore the hidden costs, Gupta breaks down his ReviewDebt framework using real pull request examples and dashboards that reveal patterns teams are missing amid the AI shift.
31.)
remobi.app: Don’t Change Your Terminal Workflow For Mobile
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Showcasing the experience, AI Engineer walks viewers through managing terminal workflows on remobi.app without sacrificing familiar commands or navigation. The walkthrough highlights features such as agent swiping and solutions for unblocking stalled workflows on mobile.
32.)
What Does Done Even Mean? Agents And Paperclip’s Liveness Model – Dotta, Paperclip
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Cutting through the hype around agentic workflows, Dotta examines the evolving meaning of “done” when bots can outpace human reviewers. Drawing from Paperclip’s liveness model, Dotta explores how risk-based review and explicit trust protocols can help teams avoid endless approval cycles and ensure AI-driven outputs are reliably actionable.
33.)
Almost Timely News: 🗞️ How To Do Feature Engineering With AI (2026-07-12)
| Christopher Penn | YouTube.com | July 12, 2026
Showcasing real-world survey techniques, Christopher Penn demonstrates how AI-powered feature engineering can turn even sparse data into actionable audience insights. By leveraging company information from email domains and applying the 5P framework, he illustrates a workflow that minimizes intrusive forms and still drives detailed segmentation for marketers.
July 11, 2026 📅️
34.)
AI Companies Want to Water Down Australia’s Copyright Laws. Artists Are Outraged, Labor Is Split
| TheGuardian.com | July 11, 2026
Tech giants are pressuring the Albanese government to relax Australia’s copyright laws in exchange for datacentre investment, prompting fierce opposition from creators and a split within Labor. Ministers Tim Ayres and Andrew Charlton are open to AI incentives, while Michelle Rowland and Tony Burke push to safeguard creative rights. No resolution or policy change has been announced.
35.)
Datacentres Drive Up Big Tech’s Carbon Emissions to a Third of Those of France
| TheGuardian.com | July 11, 2026
Carbon emissions from Microsoft, Amazon, and Google jumped nearly 18% year-over-year, reaching 119 million metric tonnes of CO2 equivalent–one-third of France’s annual total. The spike is tied to heavy datacentre construction for AI and cloud growth. UCL’s Cecilia Rikap called tech firms’ sustainability claims a marketing strategy as emissions rise despite net zero pledges.
36.)
AI ‘Actor’ Tilly Norwood Has a Movie Coming Out. Spare Us This Future
| TheGuardian.com | July 11, 2026
Particle6 has announced development on “Misaligned,” a feature film starring AI-generated actress Tilly Norwood in a narrative centered on digital sentience and emotion. Writer Dave Schilling questions whether an AI-led production can deliver genuine human connection, likening the project to propaganda rather than art. The film blends human film professionals with AI specialists.
37.)
Claws Out: Securing And Building With OpenClaw – Nick Taylor, Pomerium
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Showcasing the practical steps for locking down critical developer workflows, Nick Taylor details how he fortified OpenClaw and McClaw by contributing trusted-proxy authentication to the OpenClaw project. Sharing the real-world building of Clawspace, Taylor illustrates how a hardened environment enables powerful, browser-based tools for modern workspace security.
38.)
Stop AI Agent Hallucinations: 5 Techniques + Production Patterns – Elizabeth Fuentes, AWS
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Showcasing production-level patterns, Elizabeth Fuentes reveals how faulty agent architecture leads to costly hallucinations and inconsistent results. By dissecting five practical techniques and measuring real-world impact, Fuentes sets a clear benchmark for what robust AI agents look like in Python workflows.
39.)
The Factory That Dreams: 39 AI Agents, No Framework – Rushabh Doshi, Machinecraft
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Showcasing how a traditional machinery manufacturer built its company brain, Rushabh Doshi walks viewers through Ira, a multi-agent AI system that coordinates everything from sales to production using 39 specialized agents. The presentation details why generic AI assistants are no match for solutions architected around an organization’s own workflows, memory, and values.
40.)
Chat And Citations Won’t Save Your Vertical AI – Atul Ramachandran, Filed Inc
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Analytically unpacking the challenges of vertical AI in SaaS, Atul Ramachandran surfaces how stubborn user pain points remain, even as accuracy metrics climb. He reveals why simply adding chat interfaces or citation features shifts verification burdens back to users, arguing that real transformation hinges on deeply embedding AI in existing workflows.
41.)
State Of The Union: Why Local, Why Now — NVIDIA, Osmantic, Roboflow, EXO Labs, @matthew_berman
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Analytically framing the rapid expansion of local AI, Nader Khalil leads this panel to dissect what it means for sovereignty, privacy, and control as intelligence moves beyond the cloud. With voices from Roboflow, EXO Labs, and r/LocalLLaMA, the discussion drills into open source’s role, cost considerations, and the challenges facing widespread adoption.
42.)
Every Solo Agent Builder Eventually Reinvents A Worse Version Of CI/CD – Sumaiya Shrabony
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Analytically exposing the pitfalls of solo agent projects, Sumaiya Shrabony reveals how building agent workflows in a vacuum can regress to brittle, unreliable engineering practices. By dissecting an open-source Claude Code agent system, Shrabony argues that the real missing piece in agent development is robust infrastructure to monitor, test, and contain silent failures before they reach production.
43.)
Develop At Idea Velocity – Jeffrey Lee-Chan, Snapchat
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Analytically framing the challenge of scaling production AI agents, Jeffrey Lee-Chan cracks open the architectural pitfalls that cripple iterative task success. Drawing on thousands of hours coordinating multi-agent fleets, Lee-Chan reveals why orchestrator logic and memory persistence are now the make-or-break factors in transforming language models into reliably parallel teammates.
44.)
From Writing Code To Designing Systems: How The Developer Role Is Changing — Chris Noring, Microsoft
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Showcasing the evolution of software development, Chris Noring outlines how the rise of AI is transforming developers into planners and system architects. He breaks down how structuring intent, architectural patterns, and workflows is becoming essential for building robust solutions at scale.
45.)
Special Topics In Kernels, RL, Reward Hacking In Agents — Daniel Han, Unsloth
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Analytically parsing the frontiers of kernel methods and reinforcement learning, Daniel Han explores the nuances of reward hacking in advanced AI agents. Drawing from concepts introduced in recent AIE workshops, Han highlights subtle challenges practitioners face as agent complexity increases.
46.)
Design Patterns For AI Trust: Juries, Libraries, And Agent Tiers — Alex Bauer, Upside.tech
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Showcasing practical trust frameworks, Alex Bauer walks viewers through Upside’s strategies for managing AI reliability, from multi-agent jury models to knowledge librarian layers. With live demonstrations and the unvarnished failures that shaped these solutions, Bauer highlights how adopting proven human oversight tactics can reframe the risks teams face.
47.)
Meet The Family Running A Cereal Business From Their Dining Room With GPT-5.6
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Sitting down with the news that the Wishingrads launched a cereal company from their dining table, OpenAI pulls apart the strategic angles behind their use of GPT-5.6. The segment explores how the family integrates advanced language models into small business operations, offering a real-world look at tech-driven entrepreneurship.
48.)
On AI And Knowledge — Pablo Castro, Distinguished Engineer & CVP For AI Knowledge, Microsoft
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 11, 2026
Showcasing the intricacies of AI-driven knowledge systems, Pablo Castro breaks down how advanced information retrieval reshapes modern applications and agents. Drawing from his work across Foundry IQ, Azure AI Search, and Azure Content Understanding, Castro highlights the evolving demands of state-of-the-art AI infrastructure.
July 10, 2026 📅️
49.)
OpenAI Turns ChatGPT Into a Work Agent
| TheNeuronDaily.com | July 10, 2026
OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 and ChatGPT Work, transforming ChatGPT into a desktop agent for planning, browsing, editing files, and producing deliverables across devices. Codex was rebranded as ChatGPT Desktop, drawing mixed reactions from the developer community regarding UI and branding. Meta released Muse Spark 1.1 with a 1M-token context window and lower pricing, while Anthropic and others react to steep price competition and shifting UX expectations.
50.)
Meta Turns Off the Instagram Feature That Let Users Make AI Deepfakes of Public Accounts
| TheVerge.com | July 10, 2026
Following major backlash, Meta has disabled an Instagram feature that allowed users to generate AI images using content from public accounts by tagging them. Critics, including Haley McNamara from the National Center on Sexual Exploitation, raised concerns about misuse and privacy. The Screen Actors Guild also recommended members opt out before the shutdown.
51.)
Apple Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Trade Secret Theft
| TechCrunch.com | July 10, 2026
Apple has filed suit against OpenAI, alleging a pattern of trade secret theft orchestrated by senior OpenAI leadership, including former Apple VP Tang Tan. The complaint claims confidential Apple product details were used to recruit employees and guide OpenAI’s hardware development, as well as instances of ex-Apple staff downloading proprietary technical documents and sharing them during the hiring process.
52.)
Would You Host Part of an AI Data Center in Your Home?
| TheVerge.com | July 10, 2026
Sunrun is piloting a distributed AI compute program that installs small compute nodes in customers’ homes equipped with its solar and battery storage systems. Customers will be compensated for hosting nodes, whose compute power will be sold to enterprise buyers like AI companies. With 1.1 million customers, Sunrun aims to complete the pilot in the coming months before considering a broader rollout amid public opposition to building new centralized data centers.
53.)
Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If You Don’t Like AI, ‘Then You Shouldn’t Have It in Your Feed’
| TheVerge.com | July 10, 2026
Instagram head Adam Mosseri maintains that AI-generated content should not be filtered out entirely but should at least be clearly labeled, letting users decide what appears in their feed. Mosseri argued that while filtering is not feasible, Instagram’s AI detection will be challenged as generative models improve, and labeling “real” camera-captured content might be essential. The platform’s latest feature, Muse Spark, enables users to tag others into AI-created images, raising concerns over misuse.
54.)
OpenAI Just Rebuilt ChatGPT
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
In this breaking episode, Igor reviews OpenAI’s sweeping overhaul of ChatGPT, spotlighting the fusion of Codex and ChatGPT within the new Work app, updated voice mode, and the rollout of the GPT-5.6 family. These updates mark a key moment as OpenAI accelerates its push to unify conversational and code-focused AI tools under a single platform.
55.)
Understanding Is The New Bottleneck — Geoffrey Litt, Notion
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
Analytical in approach, Geoffrey Litt breaks down why human understanding remains a major roadblock even as autonomous AI agents surge in popularity. Drawing on lessons from education and cognitive science, Litt highlights ways to guide agentic tasks more precisely by developing practical frameworks for oversight and comprehension.
56.)
Stampli Scales Product Marketing With ChatGPT Work
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
Showcasing real-world workflow gains, Melad Zahedi outlines how leveraging ChatGPT Work has enabled Stampli to transform its product marketing output. By seamlessly connecting sources like Jira, GitHub, and meeting notes, Zahedi drives the creation of hundreds of content assets each week while freeing up bandwidth for strategy.
57.)
ChatGPT Computer Use: Now Faster With Live Picture-In-Picture
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
Showcasing the revamped Computer Use on ChatGPT Work, OpenAI demonstrates speedier performance and improved accuracy powered by GPT-5.6. The update introduces a Live Picture-in-Picture view, letting users monitor tasks in real time or even push live updates to their Pet while handling other workstreams.
58.)
Should AI Engineers Still Read Code In 2026? The Z/L Continuum — Alex Volkov, ThursdAI
| AI Engineer | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
Analytically untangling the code review debate, Alex Volkov breaks down why leading AI engineers are splitting over whether to ever read code again. He spotlights the rift between high-speed agent output and calls for meticulous human oversight, framing the Z/L Continuum as a new barometer for practical engineering risk. Volkov explains how knowing where your own workflow lives on that spectrum is now the heart of coding with (and for) AI.
59.)
Building The Future Of Agentic Infrastructure
| Claude | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
Analytical framing this discussion, Jess Yann sits down with Katelyn Lesse and Angela Jiang to dissect the demands of agentic infrastructure at production scale. Their conversation covers essential topics like agent communication, permissions, and optimizing human-agent collaboration as platform capabilities mature.
60.)
General Relativity From First Principles – Adam Brown
| Dwarkesh Patel | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
Cutting through the theoretical mystique, Dwarkesh Patel invites Adam Brown to demystify the elegant foundations of general relativity and unpack why Einstein’s core insight remains as startling as ever. The discussion moves from pivotal “coincidences” in physics to what black holes tell us about our universe, before shifting gears to the latest thinking on AI and scientific reasoning.
61.)
Can NVIDIA Keep Its Lead? — With Anissa Gardizy, Max Cherney, And Lauren Goode
| Alex Kantrowitz | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
Sitting down with leading technology reporters at the Big Technology AI Summit, Alex Kantrowitz unpacks the pressure points shaping NVIDIA’s continued dominance in the AI hardware race. The conversation spotlights deep uncertainties around data center buildouts, geopolitical risks in Taiwan, and how shifting industry priorities could threaten the current order.
62.)
RingCentral Scales Customer Programs With ChatGPT Work
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | July 10, 2026
Showcasing a real-world application, Vaneet Seth details how ChatGPT Work enabled his team at RingCentral to effortlessly expand customer program tracking from a handful to dozens of accounts. Seth highlights the practical impact of streamlined information-sharing across siloed teams, demonstrating tools that bring scattered knowledge into executable workflows.
